kennelTipLean.note: Most of the Kennel reckon we're cooked, but there's a stubborn minority calling it the turning point because 'surely it can't get worse'.

kennelTipQuotes.quote: Either this is the turning point of our season or we cop a 50-point drubbing. No in-between. — Moedogg

debates.question: Salmon's named at 13 — does he hold the lock spot for the full 80?
debates.kennel: The 'why we've regressed' thread has half the Kennel screaming that Salmon at 13 is killing our attack.

options.label: Gets hooked early, Mann finishes at 13
Shifts into the middle, Preston rolls back to lock

debates.question: First middle off the bench — who gets the early call against Utoikamanu and King?
debates.kennel: Forum's been screaming 'shit middles' all week — bench rotation is going to matter the second the Storm pack rolls forward.

options.label: Jack Underhill gets the early call

trivia.question: Which year did the Bulldogs win their most recent NRL premiership?
trivia.explainer: Canterbury beat the Roosters 16-13 in the 2004 Grand Final — Andrew Ryan's mob and still the club's last title.

recap.question: Who racked up the most run metres for the Dogs in the Round 11 hiding by the Sharks?
recap.explainer: Tuala led the Dogs with 216 run metres and chimed in with 2 line breaks — one of the only positives from a 38-16 loss.

washup.headline: 38-16 at Suncorp and the wheels are properly off.
washup.vibe: Conceded four tries in the opening half hour and the game was cooked before the meat tray was raffled. Missed 35 tackles, Sharks barely had to break a sweat after the 30-minute mark. Sits us 14th heading into a must-win against the Storm, which sounds like a sentence written by someone who hates us.
washup.kennelSummary: It's full meltdown mode — Ciraldo's job thread cracked 500 replies, the 'why have we regressed' thread is basically group therapy, and the recurring villains are Hayward's passing, Salmon at 13 and Tracey at fullback. A handful of voices begging for calm until the full-strength side is back, but they're getting drowned out.

kennelHotTakes.quote: Shit coach, shit spine, shit backs, shit front row, shit lock. Other than that we're flying.
kennelHotTakes.author: SARGE

kennelThreadsThisWeek.title: Round 12 Dogs vs Storm Friday 8pm
kennelThreadsThisWeek.summary: Gameday thread's already 491 posts deep — half the room is calling it the season-defining game, the other half is bracing for a 50-burger. Plenty of side-eye at the named 17.

kennelThreadsLastGame.title: CIRALDOS JOB FUTURE
kennelThreadsLastGame.summary: 505 replies and counting. Johnnny2233 going to bat for CC saying he just needs a proper 7, others firing back that two finals series doesn't buy you immunity from a 38-16 hiding.

kennelThreadsLastGame.title: What are your theories on why we have regressed so much this year?
kennelThreadsLastGame.summary: Big group therapy session — getting rid of Reed, Sexton, JT and Foxx all getting blamed, plus heavy fire at Hayward's left-to-right pass and Salmon stifling the attack at 13.

10.question: Salmon's named at 13 — does he start the game at lock?

options.label: No, Burton shifts to lock and Salmon goes bench
No, late change — Salmon out of the 17

10.question: Which edge do the Dogs target most in the first 20 minutes?

options.label: Through the middle — Galvin/Burton kicking game

11.question: Tracey's named at 1 — does he last the full 80 or get yanked early?

options.label: Shifted off fullback mid-game (Crichton or Burton goes back)

11.question: Where does the Dogs' first try come from?

options.label: Burton or Galvin set play / kick
Forward crash off a Hayward dart
Outside back off a broken-field scramble
